title: Diamondback Energy (FANG) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Diamondback Energy (FANG) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A conservative 5.0% growth rate is applied. While recent acquisitions will boost near-term cash flow, the inherent cyclicality and depletion rates of oil and gas assets warrant a modest base growth assumption.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate reflects the higher equity risk premium associated with commodity producers, offset by FANG's low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.40 and strong operating margins.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.0% terminal growth aligns with long-term inflation targets. It assumes oil and gas will remain a crucial part of the global energy mix for decades, even amidst the energy transition.

thesisParagraphs: Diamondback Energy has cemented its status as one of the premier independent exploration and production companies in the United States. Its competitive advantage stems entirely from its geographically concentrated asset base in the Permian Basin, allowing for unparalleled operational efficiency. With operating margins exceeding 32%, the company is highly profitable even in moderate oil price environments.

The valuation is highly sensitive to commodity prices, but FANG's low-cost structure provides a significant margin of safety. Management's commitment to returning a high percentage of free cash flow to shareholders via dividends and buybacks makes it an attractive income and value proposition at current levels.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 5% FCF growth rate for FANG?
faq.answer: Gemini selected a 5% FCF growth rate to balance the near-term cash flow accretion from recent acquisitions against the long-term natural decline rates inherent to shale production.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for FANG's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.5% discount rate was chosen to account for the inherent volatility in commodity markets, counterbalanced by Diamondback's strong balance sheet (0.40 Debt/Eq) and superior operating margins.

faq.question: How does FANG's debt level impact its valuation?
faq.answer: With a debt-to-equity ratio of just 0.40, FANG operates with conservative leverage. This reduces financial risk and lowers the weighted average cost of capital, favorably impacting the intrinsic value calculation.
